title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: You can say it a couple of ways, so you could say I'm going to meet up with my friend on Sunday, so meet up with, or I have plans with my friend on Sunday, or you could be more specific, I'm going to dinner with my friend on Sunday.

title: will / shall
examples: so instead of saying my body condition is not good, I would just say I don't feel well or how's your condition today?
but when I go back to the US, I will never use that word.
but if you use it a little bit more, you'll be able to see it.
we'll be back with more contents like this.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: but when I go back to the US, I will never use that word.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: You can say it a couple of ways, so you could say I'm going to meet up with my friend on Sunday, so meet up with, or I have plans with my friend on Sunday, or you could be more specific, I'm going to dinner with my friend on Sunday.
If you want to meet up with my friend, you can say it like you want to meet up with him.
so you can't just assume somebody is a foreigner based on how they look.
we would never say, oh, can I have a cola?
can I have a coke?

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: they just might sound a little bit more unnatural in the US and we might say it a little bit differently in the US.

title: have to / must
examples: because the US is so diverse, I also don't think there's many situations where I've ever had to emphasize this point.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: so instead of saying my body condition is not good, I would just say I don't feel well or how's your condition today?
I would say How do you feel today?
if I'm trying to ask somebody where they're from, I would say, were you born and raised in New York?
so in the US, we would never order a cola.
we would never say, oh, can I have a cola?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: because appointment is a promise, so if I have a appointment with my friend, I have an appointment.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: according to the definition, condition is being used correctly, but it just sounds a little bit unnatural in English.

title: have something done
examples: I have a dentist appointment.
I have a hair appointment at the salon or a nail appointment.
yes, I hear so many Koreans say, we have a shooting at 11 o'clock.
if somebody said we had a shooting at the beach yesterday, I think most people would think there was gun violence.
